name: Alan Liang
title: Student
description: I am a undergraduate student at the University of Wisconsin-Madison pursuing majors in Computer Science and Enviornmental Studies with certificates in Data Science and Game Design. I am excited to learn new skills and apply them to tackle world problems. I'm passionate about fullstack development, AI governance, and environmental justice. Let's connect and explore the different potentials of technology within our society!

work.title: Software Development Engineer Intern
responsibilities: Migrated an outdated service to a modern platform using React, TypeScript, and Express
Cut monthly support tickets by 50% by implementing new features to reduce manual workload
Delivered a full-stack, end-to-end project from design to production in an agile environment
Implemented comprehensive unit tests, achieving 95%+ test coverage to ensure code quality and stability
Set up user metrics using AWS Cloudwatch to track adoption and identify bottlenecks

work.title: Peer Mentor - COMP SCI 571: Building User interfaces
responsibilities: Engaged with students in office hours on UI/UX development tools : HTML, CSS, JS, React.js, React Native, Figma.
Guided students to develop user-centric design principles in web and mobile design
Fostered a positive learning environment in a course of 300+ students

work.title: Beach Ambassador
responsibilities: Developed the official Milwaukee Beach Ambassador Website using HTML, Wordpress, and user focused design principles
Interacted with 500+ beachgoers to spread awareness about beach safety along the Milwaukee lakefront
Engaged with the community at citywide events promoting environmental justice and education

work.title: Instructor
responsibilities: Worked with a dynamic team of instructors to support the learning of over 100 students every day
Mastered socratic questioning to guide kids towards a metacognitive understanding of mathematical processes
Actively engaged with the community at local events to spark interest in mathematics
Guided and trained new employees through the process of effectively tutoring
